description: tpm2 algorithm() returns SecretError::Backend when called on an NV-mode backend. pkcs11 algorithm() returns SecretError::Backend when the cache is cold. The error semantics differ: tpm2 errors because 'this mode does not support signing at all' (permanent), while pkcs11 errors because 'you have not called sign() yet' (transient, fixable by the caller). The trait doc (secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1414-1424) says 'If the cache is cold, algorithm() returns an error rather than blocking on HSM I/O.' The tpm2 case is not a cold cache -- it is a wrong-mode error. Consider returning a distinct error variant or documenting this distinction in the tpm2 doc comment on algorithm().
title: tpm2 algorithm() semantics diverge from pkcs11
